bead.title: docs: resolve TP-002 historical versus active status
bead.description:
PROBLEM
TP-002 says it is historical but also marks itself active. That contradiction makes it unclear whether test coverage gaps in the old React-stack plan are current obligations or retained historical notes.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:17 says the plan is historical and points to ADR-002 v2 for the current stack.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:18 says `Status: Active`.
- Deferred UI cases remain listed later in the same plan, including close/reopen/drag-drop rows around :152-154.

PROPOSED FIX
Update TP-002 status/header language so historical content is explicitly superseded, or split any still-current test obligations into a current-stack plan. Keep the change small and do not rewrite the historical body unnecessarily.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement UI close/reopen/drag-drop features. Do not rewrite ADR-002 or the current Svelte stack docs unless a direct reference needs updating.
bead.acceptance:
1. docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md no longer says both Historical and Status: Active without explanation.
2. Any still-current TP-002 obligations are pointed to a current-stack plan or explicitly scoped as retained historical reference.
3. `rg -n "Historical.*Status: Active|Status: Active" docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md` has no contradictory hit.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
